summary.business_type: Health/Wellness Content Publisher — Skincare Resource Center

top_findings.title: Site unreachable via production domain (facialcupping.com) — DNS resolves but HTTP returns empty response
List pages (Learn, How-To, Research, About) use excellent structured data with CollectionPage + ItemList + BreadcrumbList schema
No sitemap-index.xml accessible on production — Astro sitemap integration present but sitemap not found during audit
Missing hreflang tags — acceptable for single-language English site but no language annotation present
Section pages with 5+ articles have good content depth; single-article sections (editorial, contact) are thin
og:image URLs reference relative paths without full domain in some meta tags; BaseLayout properly constructs absolute URLs

quick_wins: Deploy the site to production so it is accessible from facialcupping.com
Verify sitemap-index.xml is generated during build and properly referenced
Add Article schema to individual article pages for enhanced rich results
Ensure all section pages have at least 3 articles before going live to avoid thin content penalties
Add alt text improvements to all article body images
Add lastmod dates to sitemap for better crawl efficiency

categories.name: Technical SEO
what_works: Canonical tags present on all pages with correct URLs
robots.txt properly configured with Allow for all major AI crawlers (GPTBot, Claude-Web, CCBot, Google-Extended, PerplexityBot)
Meta robots 'index, follow' correctly applied on all pages
Responsive viewport meta tag present
Clean URL structure with no query parameters
Proper HTTP to HTTPS (when deployed)

findings.title: Production site is not accessible
findings.description: DNS resolves (67.228.37.8) but HTTP requests return empty/000 responses. Site is only accessible via local dev server.
findings.recommendation: Investigate hosting/Cloudflare/SSL configuration. Ensure the Astro build is deployed correctly.

findings.title: Sitemap-index.xml returns 404
findings.description: robots.txt references https://facialcupping.com/sitemap-index.xml but this endpoint returns 404. Astro sitemap integration is configured but the build output may not generate the correct sitemap path.
findings.recommendation: Check astro.config.mjs sitemap configuration. The default @astrojs/sitemap generates /sitemap-index.xml but build may need verification.

findings.title: No HTTP security headers observed
findings.description: Security headers (X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, Content-Security-Policy, Strict-Transport-Security) were not observed in responses.
findings.recommendation: Configure security headers at the hosting/CDN level. HSTS preload recommended for HTTPS enforcement.

findings.title: No 404 page detected
findings.description: No custom 404 page was defined in the source code. Astro's default 404 will be shown.
findings.recommendation: Create a custom src/pages/404.astro with helpful navigation and search.

findings.title: Script loading uses module/nomodule pattern from Vite
findings.description: All scripts loaded via Vite dev server with module type. CSS bundled inline in dev mode.
findings.recommendation: Ensure production build splits CSS and JS appropriately with code splitting.

categories.name: Content Quality
what_works: Well-researched articles with scientific citations and references to PubMed studies
Author attribution with credentials (Sarah Chen, Licensed Esthetician)
Clear E-E-A-T signals: editorial guidelines, review process, mission page, author bylines
Comprehensive content depth — 'What Is Facial Cupping' article is 157 lines with tables, sections, and references
Regular content updates (dateUpdated field used consistently)
Good internal linking between related articles

findings.title: Single-article sections risk thin content
findings.description: Several sections have only 1-2 articles: /about/editorial, /about/contact, /about/review-process, /oils/oils. CollectionPage schema with one item looks thin to search engines.
findings.recommendation: Aim for minimum 3 articles per section before publishing. Consolidate thin sections or mark as noindex until sufficient content exists.

findings.title: Some list pages lack long descriptions
findings.description: Not all sections have a `longDescription` field. Sections like 'brands', 'comparisons' use short descriptions imported from section config, which are too brief for effective indexing.
findings.recommendation: Add longDescription to every section's markdown frontmatter to provide rich context on list pages.

findings.title: About section articles lack author bylines
findings.description: Articles like contact.md and editorial.md use the default author 'FacialCupping.com Team' rather than named individuals.
findings.recommendation: Assign named authors with credentials to all content for stronger E-E-A-T signals.

findings.title: No article word count analysis performed
findings.description: Article lengths vary significantly. Some articles may fall below Google's quality threshold for thin content.
findings.recommendation: Audit all articles for minimum 600+ words. Consider expanding shorter pieces.

categories.name: On-Page SEO
what_works: All 11 section list pages have unique, descriptive title tags with section-level keywords
Meta descriptions are unique per section and include relevant keywords
H1 headings match section titles and are present on every page
Clean semantic HTML structure with <nav>, <main>, <header>, <footer>
Breadcrumb navigation on every list page for clear site architecture
Article cards use H3 headings for each article title within the list grid
Canonical URLs correctly set on all pages

findings.title: Title tag format inconsistent with branding
findings.description: List pages use format '{Title} | FacialCupping.com' but homepage uses '{Title} | FacialCupping.com' (same). Consider keeping consistent 'primary keyword | FacialCupping.com' format.
findings.recommendation: Standardize title format: 'Primary Keyword | FacialCupping.com' across all pages.

findings.title: Article detail pages may not have optimized titles
findings.description: Article detail pages use the article title directly as the H1. Some titles may exceed optimal 60-character limit for SERP display.
findings.recommendation: Review article titles and ensure they stay under 60 characters where possible.

findings.title: No Open Graph article:author or article:published_time tags
findings.description: BaseLayout sets og:type only to 'website'. For article pages, it should use 'article' type with additional article meta tags.
findings.recommendation: Add og:type='article' and article:published_time / article:author for article detail pages.

findings.title: Internal linking could be stronger between related list pages
findings.description: List pages have limited cross-linking between sections. No 'Related Sections' module on list pages to interlink topics.
findings.recommendation: Add a 'Related Sections' widget to the bottom of each list page linking to complementary sections.

categories.name: Schema / Structured Data
what_works: WebSite schema with SearchAction on every page via BaseLayout
CollectionPage + ItemList schema on every list page with full article listings
BreadcrumbList JSON-LD on every list page with Home > Section hierarchy
Correct @context and @type usage throughout
ItemList includes position, name, description, and URL for each article
SearchAction target uses correct URL pattern

findings.title: Article schema missing on detail pages
findings.description: Individual article pages ([slug].astro) do not include Article or NewsArticle schema markup. This is a missed opportunity for rich results like 'Article' rich snippets.
findings.recommendation: Add Article schema (with @type: Article, headline, author, datePublished, dateModified, image, publisher) to all article detail pages.

findings.title: No FAQ schema on FAQ pages
findings.description: The FAQ article at /learn/facial-cupping-faq contains Q&A content but does not mark it up with FAQPage schema.
findings.recommendation: Add FAQPage schema to articles containing structured Q&A content for rich snippet eligibility.

findings.title: No Organization/LocalBusiness schema
findings.description: No Organization schema exists for the brand itself. Publisher information not available in structured data.
findings.recommendation: Add Organization schema with name, logo, URL, and sameAs social profiles.

findings.title: WebSite SearchAction search target may not function
findings.description: SearchAction targets /?q={search_term_string} but no search functionality exists on the site.
findings.recommendation: Either implement a search feature or use a SearchAction target compatible with Google site search.

categories.name: Performance (CWV)
what_works: Minimal third-party scripts — only React hydration and Vite dev server
Static content pre-rendered by Astro for fast initial load
Eager loading on hero images with fetchpriority='high'
Font files use woff2 format with appropriate unicode-range subsets
Tailwind CSS used (smaller CSS output)

findings.title: Images not optimized for performance
findings.description: Hero images loaded at full resolution (e.g., hero-bg.webp at 94KB) without responsive image srcset. Large images will impact LCP on mobile.
findings.recommendation: Implement responsive images using Astro's <Image /> or <Picture /> component with srcset and sizes.

findings.title: CSS bundled with Vite in dev mode
findings.description: All Tailwind styles are inlined in the HTML head during development. Production build should extract to separate CSS file.
findings.recommendation: Verify production build outputs optimized, minified, and code-split CSS bundles.

findings.title: No lazy loading for below-fold images
findings.description: Article body images do not use loading='lazy' attribute. All images attempt to load on page render.
findings.recommendation: Add loading='lazy' to all below-the-fold images in article content.

findings.title: No offline/mobile performance data available
findings.description: CWV Lab and Field data were not measured. Production deployment is needed for Lighthouse testing.
findings.recommendation: Once deployed, run Lighthouse in CI and monitor CrUX data via Google Search Console.

categories.name: AI Search Readiness
what_works: llms.txt file properly configured with complete site structure and article links
robots.txt allows all major AI crawlers (GPTBot, Claude-Web, Google-Extended, PerplexityBot, CCBot)
Clear, authoritative content with scientific citations improves citability
Clean HTML structure with semantic elements aids AI comprehension
Author credential signals (licensed esthetician) boost E-E-A-T for AI systems
Structured data (CollectionPage, BreadcrumbList) helps AI understand content relationships

findings.title: llms.txt lacks section descriptions
findings.description: llms.txt only lists page titles and URLs without section summaries or context descriptions that help AI crawlers understand content relevance.
findings.recommendation: Add brief section descriptions after each ## heading in llms.txt to provide context.

findings.title: No llms-full.txt available
findings.description: Only basic llms.txt is implemented. llms-full.txt with full article text would improve AI comprehension and citation accuracy.
findings.recommendation: Generate llms-full.txt containing complete article content for AI crawlers.

findings.title: Article content not structured for passage-level citation
findings.description: While content is well-written, it does not use explicit IDs or anchors on sections for direct citation.
findings.recommendation: Add anchor IDs to all section headings (h2, h3) to enable AI crawlers to cite specific passages.

categories.name: Images
what_works: All images use modern WebP format for better compression
OG images are unique per section with descriptive filenames (learn.webp, howto.webp, etc.)
Alt text present on hero images (using title as alt text)
Favicon in multiple formats (svg, ico, apple-touch-icon)

findings.title: Hero image alt text uses title instead of descriptive alternative text
findings.description: PageHero component uses `alt={title}` which sets the page title as image alt text. Alt text should describe the image content.
findings.recommendation: Change alt text to describe the image (e.g., 'Woman performing facial cupping therapy with silicone cups on cheeks').

findings.title: Missing image dimensions on article body images
findings.description: Images in article prose do not have explicit width/height attributes, risking Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS).
findings.recommendation: Add width and height attributes to all elements in article content.

findings.title: No responsive image srcset
findings.description: All images serve a single resolution regardless of viewport. A 1280px hero image is served to mobile devices unnecessarily.
findings.recommendation: Implement Astro's Image component with srcset/sizes for responsive image delivery.

findings.title: Large image file sizes detected
findings.description: Some hero images are large (hero-bg.webp: 58KB, research.webp: 86KB, safety.webp: 76KB). These could be further compressed.
findings.recommendation: Compress WebP images further using tools like squoosh or sharp. Target <40KB for hero images.

items.action: Deploy site to production — fix DNS/SSL/hosting configuration for facialcupping.com
Verify sitemap-index.xml generation and accessibility
Add Article schema markup to all article detail pages ([slug].astro)
Create custom 404 page
Implement responsive images with srcset/sizes using Astro <Image /> component
Fix hero image alt text to be descriptive
Implement lazy loading for below-the-fold images
Add longDescription to all sections for better context on list pages
Add anchor IDs to all h2/h3 headings for AI passage citation
Expand llms.txt with section descriptions
Add Organization schema markup
Expand single-article sections to minimum 3 articles each
Audit all article word counts; expand articles under 600 words
Add FAQPage schema to Q&A content
Assign named author credentials to all articles
Add article OG meta tags (og:type:article, article:published_time, article:author)
Add cross-linking between related sections on list pages
Set up Google Search Console / Bing Webmaster Tools once deployed
Run Lighthouse CI on every deployment
Monitor Core Web Vitals via CrUX data
Set up content freshness alerts for articles with dateUpdated
Consider implementing search functionality for SearchAction schema
